ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-05-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE WAS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 7/23/07 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 7/13/07 @ 9:30AM IN DEPT. 304. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 5/3/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 5/24/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: D. LOK; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE CSR #4280 (206)

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Apportion Settlement Funds.
Off Calendar Sep-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Motion for an Apportionment Proceeding and an Order to Distribute Settlement Funds Hearing.
Dept. 218, Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr., Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.
Young S. Lee, Esq. for and with plaintiff Janine Fout.
Victoria, Jessica and James D. Fout, the heirs of decedent Junior D. Fout are present in pro per, parties were placed under oath and gave testimony on behalf of themselves.
After hearing the testimonies presented, the matter was taken under submission for review.
Proposed Order received in court.

P.M.
The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the testimony and evidence, both oral and documentary, good cause appearing, the Court granted the motion in the following proportions:
Janine Fout 50%, Victoria Fout 20%, Jessica Fout 20% and James D. Fout 10%.
Attorneys' Fees and costs in the sum of $1,093.37
Future net settlement funds shall be distributed to the decedent's heirs in the percentages set forth in this order.
Order signed by Judge Richard Ulmer, Jr.
